title: coelogyne xanthobulbon
intro: Coelogyne xanthobulbon, also known as the golden yellow orchid, is a species of orchid native to the tropical forests of Southeast Asia. It is a terrestrial orchid, meaning it grows on the ground, and is prized for its unique yellow flowers and medicinal properties. The plant contains compounds such as flavonoids and alkaloids, which have been found to have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, making it a potential treatment for various health conditions, including fever, cough, and respiratory issues. Traditionally, the plant is used in herbal preparations, such as teas and infusions, and is often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
